channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Exploring Junk Journaling: A Creative Way to Document Life's Moments
item.description: Junk journaling is a stress-free alternative to traditional journaling that uses everyday found items and ephemera to document daily experiences. Unlike conventional methods, it allows for creativity ...

item.title: Senate probes NWDC governance issues, financial mismanagement
item.description: The Senate Committee on Regional Development probed the North West Development Commission (NWDC) over governance challenges, financial mismanagement, and delayed appointments of executive directors. L...

item.title: Navigating Financial Support for Adult Children in Australia's Economic Climate
item.description: The article explores the financial challenges faced by younger Australians in their 30s, struggling with mortgage and living costs amid rising interest rates. Older Australians in their 60s, with stab...

item.title: Scientists Develop Programmable Material to Control Heat
item.description: Researchers at Osaka Metropolitan University have created a groundbreaking material that can control and 'program' heat, enabling precise thermal radiation direction, mode switching, and memory retent...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Property Boundaries and Neighborly Rights
item.description: The article discusses a legal dispute between neighbors regarding property boundaries and the right to paint one's side of a shared fence. A consumer lawyer, DEAN DUNHAM KC, provides advice on whether...

item.title: Blue Jays Struggle to Turn Around Season Amid Injuries and Slump
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays are facing a challenging season, with frustrations mounting as they struggle to improve their performance. Entering a critical phase of the season, the team has lost four consecu...

item.title: California DMV identifies anomalies in 11,000 driver tests, prompting license retakes
item.description: California's Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) has identified anomalies in the results of certain knowledge tests, prompting approximately 11,000 drivers to retake their written exams within the next...

item.title: CNN Panel Debates Harriet Tubman's Placement on $20 Bill, With Trump Supporters and Democrats Clash
item.description: A heated debate erupted on a CNN panel regarding the decision to replace Andrew Jackson's face on the $20 bill with Harriet Tubman, a historical figure known for leading enslaved people to freedom via...

item.title: Super Typhoon Inday Enters PAR with Wind Signal No. 1 in 7 Provinces
item.description: The article highlights the entry of Super Typhoon Inday into the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR), triggering wind signal no. 1 in seven provinces. It details the typhoon's impact, including po...

item.title: B.C. Nurses Continue Strike Action Amid Ongoing Dispute
item.description: B.C. nurses have resumed strike actions, including a picket line at Vancouver General Hospital, following the rejection of a proposed deal by two-thirds of union members. The BC Nurses’ Union began jo...

item.title: Olivia Attwood and Pete Wicks enjoy a romantic getaway in Ibiza
item.description: The article details Olivia Attwood's recent activities during her Ibiza holiday, highlighting her passionate kiss with boyfriend Pete Wicks and her arrival at a dinner date with a designer bag. It als...

item.title: Kenyan Police Officer Completes Training in Australia
item.description: A former Kenyan police officer, Meshack Mtumishi, has successfully completed his training at the Queensland Police Service academy in Brisbane, Australia, marking a significant career milestone. The 3...

item.title: FIFA Suspends US Soccer Officials for Protocol Violations in Belgium World Cup Match
item.description: FIFA suspended two U.S. Soccer Federation officials, Sam Zapatka and Frank Pannell, from Monday's World Cup match against Belgium due to alleged protocol violations. The suspensions were linked to inc...

item.title: Canada Province Considers Legal Action Against OpenAI Over School Shooting Incident
item.description: A Canadian province is reportedly preparing to file a lawsuit against OpenAI, the company behind the widely used artificial intelligence model, over its alleged role in a school shooting incident. The...

item.title: Supreme Court Dismisses DMK's Plea Against Vijay Over Political Allegations
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has rejected the plea filed by the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) party against Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin, dismissing allegations that the judiciary is being mi...

item.title: ICE officer shot dead Mexican immigrant during Houston traffic stop amid immigration enforcement tensions
item.description: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer has fatally shot a Mexican immigrant in Houston during a traffic stop, according to federal authorities. The incident occurred on Tuesday when Lore...

item.title: Switzerland advances to FIFA World Cup quarter-finals after penalty shootout victory over Colombia
item.description: In a thrilling encounter during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Switzerland secured their place in the quarter-finals by defeating Colombia 4-3 in a penalty shootout after a goalless draw. The match, played ...

item.title: Egypt Coach Alleges Unfair Treatment After World Cup Exit
item.description: Egypt's coach, Hossam Hassan, accused officials of bias after his team's dramatic 3-2 defeat to Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Despite leading 2-0, Argentina staged a late comeback through Enzo...

item.title: Cape Town Faces Systemic Service Delivery Failures and Remedial Actions
item.description: The Public Protector Kholeka Gcaleka has found that the City of Cape Town's failure to deliver basic municipal services to Langa Flats and Khayelitsha residents constitutes maladministration. The inve...

item.title: Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's funeral ceremonies begin in Iraq and Qom
item.description: The funeral processions for late Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei have commenced, with his remains transported to Iraq for public ceremonies. Khamenei, who died in February during US-Israeli strike...
